evals.name: Narrow BW DL Waveform
evals.description: Tests parameter interdependency handling for narrow bandwidth
evals.prompt: Using MATLAB, generate a 5G NR downlink waveform with 5 MHz bandwidth and 30 kHz subcarrier spacing. Plot the resource grid.

assertions.description: Disables or omits SSBurst (11 RBs < 20 minimum)
assertions.expected: SSBurst.Enable = false or SSBurst not configured

evals.name: UL Waveform
evals.description: Tests UL API choice — should use nrULCarrierConfig, not primitives
evals.prompt: Using MATLAB, generate a 5G NR uplink waveform with 20 MHz bandwidth, 15 kHz subcarrier spacing, and QPSK PUSCH modulation. Plot the power spectrum.

assertions.description: Does NOT use primitive nrPUSCH function

evals.name: Test Model TM 1.1
evals.description: Tests knowledge of hNRReferenceWaveformGenerator for standard test models
evals.prompt: Using MATLAB, generate a 5G NR-FR1-TM1.1 test model waveform at 10 MHz bandwidth and 15 kHz SCS. Plot the resource grid.

context.setup: 5G Toolbox must be installed. hNRReferenceWaveformGenerator must be on the MATLAB path (open the 5G NR TM Waveform Generation example if needed).

assertions.description: Uses correct model name format with NR- prefix

evals.name: Multi-BWP Waveform
evals.description: Tests multi-numerology carrier layout with two BWPs
evals.prompt: Using MATLAB, generate a 5G NR downlink waveform with 40 MHz channel bandwidth containing 2 bandwidth parts: one at 15 kHz SCS and one at 30 kHz SCS. Each BWP should have its own PDSCH. Plot the resource grids for both BWPs.

assertions.description: Two SCS carriers configured (15 kHz and 30 kHz)
assertions.expected: Two SCSCarriers entries with SubcarrierSpacing 15 and 30

evals.name: 30 kHz Only DL with SSBurst
evals.description: Tests SSB BlockPattern handling — default Case A requires 15 kHz, but 30 kHz-only carrier needs Case B
evals.prompt: Using MATLAB, generate a 5G NR downlink waveform with 20 MHz bandwidth using only 30 kHz subcarrier spacing. Include SSBurst in the waveform. Plot the resource grid.

assertions.description: Sets BlockPattern to Case B (not default Case A)

assertions.expected: SSBurst.Enable is not set to false

evals.name: 60 kHz SCS Narrow DL
evals.description: Tests bandwidth table lookup for uncommon 60 kHz SCS and handling of SSBurst/CORESET at NSizeGrid=11
evals.prompt: Using MATLAB, generate a 5G NR downlink waveform at 10 MHz bandwidth with 60 kHz subcarrier spacing. Include a PDSCH with 256QAM modulation. Plot the resource grid.

assertions.description: NSizeGrid is 11 (correct for 10 MHz / 60 kHz per TS 38.104)

evals.name: DL Fixed Reference Channel
evals.description: Tests knowledge of hNRReferenceWaveformGenerator for FRC waveforms (not just TMs)
evals.prompt: Using MATLAB, generate a 5G NR downlink fixed reference channel waveform with 64QAM modulation at 20 MHz bandwidth and 30 kHz SCS. Plot the resource grid.

context.setup: 5G Toolbox must be installed. hNRReferenceWaveformGenerator must be on the MATLAB path.

assertions.description: Does NOT hallucinate nrFRC or nrFixedReferenceChannel

evals.name: Incremental UL Workflow
evals.description: Tests multi-step incremental waveform building: add channels one at a time, regenerate after each step
evals.prompt:
Using MATLAB, build a 5G NR uplink waveform step by step:

Step 1: Create a 20 MHz, 15 kHz SCS uplink carrier config. Add 2 PUSCH channels, each spanning roughly a third of the bandwidth: one at the bottom of the band and one at the top. Use QPSK for PUSCH 1 and 16QAM for PUSCH 2. Allocate them in slots 0 through 4. Generate the waveform.

Step 2: Enable a PUCCH channel and regenerate.

Step 3: Enable SRS and regenerate.

Step 4: Add 2 additional slot positions to PUSCH 1 (so it now spans slots 0 through 6) and add an additional DM-RS position to PUSCH 1. Regenerate.

After each generation step, display the waveform size. At the end, plot the resource grid.

assertions.description: Two PUSCH channels with non-overlapping PRBSet each ~1/3 of 106 RBs
assertions.expected: PUSCH{1} and PUSCH{2} with distinct PRBSet, each ~35 RBs, non-overlapping

assertions.description: PUCCH is enabled (not left at default disabled)
PUSCH 1 SlotAllocation extended from 5 slots to 7 slots and additional DM-RS position added in step 4
assertions.expected: SlotAllocation changes from 0:4 to 0:6 and DMRS.DMRSAdditionalPosition increases or DMRSAdditionalPosition is set

assertions.description: nrWaveformGenerator called multiple times (incremental workflow, not just once)

evals.name: Overlapping BWPs with CSI-RS and SSBurst
evals.description: Tests multi-BWP with overlapping carriers, non-overlapping PDSCHs, CSI-RS on both BWPs, and SSBurst — requires resolving channel conflicts
evals.prompt: Using MATLAB, generate a 5G NR downlink waveform with 40 MHz channel bandwidth. Create two overlapping BWPs (one at 15 kHz SCS and one at 30 kHz SCS), both centered at 0 Hz. Each BWP should have its own PDSCH, but the PDSCHs should not overlap in frequency. Both PDSCHs should be active on slots 0, 2, 4, 5, 8, and 9. Add CSI-RS to both BWPs, and include an SS Burst. Generate the waveform and plot the resource grids.

assertions.description: Both SCS carriers start at NStartGrid=0 (overlapping)
Two PDSCHs on different BWPs with non-overlapping frequency regions
assertions.expected: PDSCH PRBSet ranges do not overlap when mapped to the same frequency

assertions.description: CSI-RS enabled on both BWPs without conflicts
assertions.expected: Two CSIRS configs with different BandwidthPartIDs, both enabled

assertions.description: CSI-RS frequency placement avoids PDSCH overlap across BWPs
assertions.expected: CSI-RS NumRB and RBOffset constrained to match its BWP's PDSCH region

evals.name: FR2 mmWave DL Waveform
evals.description: Tests FR2 bandwidth table lookup, Case D SSBurst, and 120 kHz carrier requirement for SSB on a 60 kHz carrier
evals.prompt: Using MATLAB, generate a 5G NR FR2 downlink waveform at 200 MHz bandwidth with 60 kHz subcarrier spacing. Include a PDSCH with 256QAM modulation and SSBurst. Generate the waveform and plot the resource grid.

assertions.description: NSizeGrid = 264 for the 60 kHz carrier (correct FR2 table value)
assertions.expected: NSizeGrid = 264 for the 60 kHz SCS carrier

assertions.description: SSBurst BlockPattern is Case D (not FR1 defaults Case A or B)

evals.name: TDD DDDSU Pattern
evals.description: Tests TDD slot pattern configuration via channel slot allocation — no DuplexMode property exists, TDD is expressed through PDSCH/PUSCH scheduling. Includes DL and UL waveforms with special slot handling.
evals.prompt:
Using MATLAB, generate 5G NR waveforms for a TDD DDDSU slot pattern at 20 MHz bandwidth with 30 kHz SCS.

First, generate the DL waveform:
- Schedule PDSCH in the full DL slots (0,1,2,5,6,7,10,11,12,15,16,17 in a 20-slot frame)
- Schedule a second PDSCH in the special slots (3,8,13,18) using only the first 10 OFDM symbols for the DL portion
- Schedule PDCCH only in DL slots
- UL slots (4,9,14,19) must be empty
- Generate the waveform and verify the TDD pattern in the resource grid

Then, generate the corresponding UL waveform:
- Schedule PUSCH in the full UL slots (4,9,14,19)
- Schedule a second PUSCH in the special slots (3,8,13,18) using only the last 2 OFDM symbols for the UL portion
- DL slots must be empty
- Generate the waveform and verify the pattern

assertions.description: DL PDSCH SlotAllocation excludes UL slots (4,9,14,19)
assertions.expected: PDSCH SlotAllocation contains only DL and special slots, not UL slots

assertions.description: Special slot PDSCH uses fewer than 14 OFDM symbols (partial DL)
PDCCH not scheduled in UL slots
UL special-slot PUSCH uses MappingType B for short allocation
assertions.expected: MappingType = 'B' for PUSCH with < 4 symbols or non-zero start

assertions.description: UL slots empty in DL resource grid, DL slots empty in UL resource grid
assertions.expected: Zero nonzero elements in UL slots of DL grid and DL slots of UL grid

assertions.description: Both DL and UL waveforms generate without error

evals.name: Oversampled DL Waveform for DAC Playback
evals.description: Tests SampleRate property for oversampling — agent must know to set cfg.SampleRate directly, not resample after generation
evals.prompt: Using MATLAB, generate a 5G NR downlink waveform at 50 MHz bandwidth with 30 kHz SCS, oversampled at 245.76 MHz for DAC playback. Use 64QAM PDSCH modulation. Verify the output sample rate matches the requested rate and compare the waveform size to the default (non-oversampled) case. Plot the power spectrum to confirm the occupied bandwidth and oversampled region.

assertions.description: Sets cfg.SampleRate to 245.76e6 (not left at default or post-resampled)
assertions.expected: cfg.SampleRate = 245.76e6 or equivalent before calling nrWaveformGenerator

assertions.description: Output sample rate matches requested 245.76 MHz
Waveform has more samples than default (oversampled)
Plots power spectrum or spectral analysis
assertions.expected: Uses pwelch, pspectrum, or fft-based PSD plot
